title: Update a Connection
content:
# Update a Connection

## Change Version

You can upgrade the Application version of a Connection.

    You can also rollback to a previous version if the current version has issues.

Go to **Connections** in the Kelvin UI.

You can either click on the relevant upgrade icon;

Or click on the Connection Name and click on the **Update Connection** button.

Select the new version you want to use.

The rest of the process is exactly the same when you first create a Connection.

    If there are any new App Parameters or App Configurations in the new version, then the default values will be automatically applied to all Assets currently deployed to the Application.

    These values can be changed during or after the upgrade is completed.

For documentation details for the rest of the steps, go to the relevant documentation page and start at **Step 3**.

* [OPC-UA Connection](../connect/connect-to-data/opc-ua.md)
* [Modbus Connection](../connect/connect-to-data/modbus.md)
* [ROC Connection](../connect/connect-to-data/roc.md)
* [CSV Publisher Connection](../connect/connect-to-data/csv-publisher.md)
* Custom Connection - Refer to the developer's documentation for details

## Update Configuration

After deployment you can change the configuration settings that you originally set.

Go to **Connections** in the Kelvin UI.

Click on the Connection name.

Click on the **Configuration** tab.

You can choose to edit in the UI (if available), in YAML or JSON format.

When ready, click on the Apply** and Redeploy** button

## Update Deployment

After deployment you can change the deployment settings that you originally set.

Go to **Connections** in the Kelvin UI.

Click on the Connection name.

Click on the **System** tab.

Here you can adjust the values, such as the environment variables, as require and then click on the **Redploy** button.

## Update Connection IO

After deployment you can change the IO Mappings that you originally set.

    You can also add or delete Assets from the IO Maps.

Go to **Connections** in the Kelvin UI.

Click on the Connection name.

Click on the **Connection IO** tab.

To edit, click on the **Edit IO Map** button.

You will then see the list of Assets that you can edit, add or delete.

When ready click the **Save** button.
